docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Bringing TV Box info to a Unified Place.

BoxBase is a Tv Box Wiki project that aims to unify obscure TV Box information in one place. Eliminating the necessity to hunt for obscure information in the World Wide Web.

Warning

Project BoxBase is a non-profit project that aims to unify TV Box information. This project does not help:

  • Installing forbidden IPTV clients
  • Praticing and/or doing any form of Piracy

docs.title: Click here to see documented Devices
Project BoxBase needs your help!
docs.text: BoxBase is a very new wiki. With a growing index of documented devices. Please help grow the number of documented devices by documenting any TV Box that you own and add a pull request to the BoxBase Github.

docs.title: HTV H8
docs.text:
Warning

The page for this device is incomplete. Be a contributor and help complete this page and many other incomplete pages on the BaseBox Project. Info in this page is not 100% acurrate.

docs.title: System Info
docs.text:
This model have a Android-Based firmware. This allows for further user customization, such as installing apps, modifying the UI, etc.

But also frequently features GMS. Wich is recommended to disable/uninstall. Because most android TV Boxes have very low-end hardware, and Google aims to block apk installing.

See more on Google apk blocking in this link.

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text: Android Version Chipset Rom & Ram DroidLogic Based? GMS Installed Developer mode acessible? 11 Red Velvet Cake (Kernel Version 5.4.x) Amlogic S905X4 16 GB & 2 GB ✅ ✅ ✅ (Needs External App)

docs.title: Enabling Developer mode
docs.text:
This TV Box model disables the conventional method of enabling developer mode (clicking 7 times in Build Number), so, a external app is needed to do this work.

We recommend using this app to do the job.

docs.title: Known Quircks
docs.text:
• Offers a built-in IPTV app.

  • Features a Bluetooth controller and Voice Controls.

  • Support for Amazon(R) Alexa(R) integration

  • Base system and apps is similar to UniTV S1 and HTV H9 (Including the IPTV Player)

docs.title: Enabling Developer mode and Shizuku
docs.text:
This TV Box model disables the conventional method of enabling developer mode (clicking 7 times in Build Number), so, a external app is needed to do this work.

We recommend using this app to do the job.

docs.title: Shizuku (Untested)
docs.text:
Thecnically, this TV Box model have proper support to setup and run Shizuku.

You can try to setup Shizuku by enabling Wireless ADB and return here to say if it works.

docs.title: Known Quircks
docs.text:
• Offers a built-in IPTV app.

  • Features a Bluetooth controller and Voice Controls.

  • Support for Amazon(R) Alexa(R) integration

  • Offers native AI image upscaling

  • Base system and apps is similar to UniTV S1 and HTV H8 (Including the IPTV Player)

docs.title: System Info
docs.text:
This model have a Android-Based firmware. This allows for further user customization, such as installing apps, modifying the UI, etc.

But also frequently features GMS. Wich is recommended to disable/uninstall. Because most android TV Boxes have very low-end hardware, and Google aims to block apk installing.

See more on Google apk blocking in this link.

docs.title: Specifications
docs.text: Android Version Chipset Rom & Ram DroidLogic Based? GMS Installed Developer mode acessible? 7.1.2 Nougat (Kernel Version 3.14.29) Amlogic S905W 8 GB & 1GB ✅ ✅ ✅ (Also Root)

docs.title: Enabling Developer mode and Built-in Rooting
docs.text:
This TV Box model does not disable the traditional way of enabling developer mode. So, use the classic route of clicking 7 times in the build number.

Note

Fun Fact: This TV Box model features a ROOT MODE SWITCH directly in advanced settings. This means that you can do deep customizations in this model, uninstall blotware from the root, and do MUCH MORE! But caution. This also heavily compromisses security and can potentialy lead to a soft/hard-brick.

docs.title: Known Quircks
docs.text:
• Kernel is based on Ubuntu.

  • Low-End hardware suffers from slowness.

  • Comes with a bunch of old services and apps installed

  • Veeeeeery slow with GMS enabled

  • The controler from This Model Sucessor (Aquário STV-3000) is a bit backwards compatible with it.

docs.title: System Info
docs.text:

docs.title: Enabling Developer mode and Built-in Rooting
docs.text:
This TV Box model does not disable the traditional way of enabling developer mode. So, use the classic route of clicking 7 times in the build number.

Note

Fun Fact: This TV Box model features a ROOT MODE SWITCH directly in advanced settings. This means that you can do deep customizations in this model, uninstall blotware from the root, and do MUCH MORE! But caution. This also heavily compromisses security and can potentialy lead to a soft/hard-brick.

docs.title: Known Quircks
docs.text:
• Kernel is based on Ubuntu.

  • Low-End hardware suffers from slowness.

  • Has a Thinner and Slimmer look compared to its predecessor (Aquário STV-2000).

  • Has the SAME CHIPSET from its predecessor. Performance improvements often came only from the firmware.
